column3: Issue
column4: Statement A
column5: Statement / instrument / record B
column6: Conflict
column7: Significance

column3: Trust mischaracterised to deny beneficiary rights
column4: O.S. 243/2024 · plaint by D2 · 14.08.2024 · para 1: trust "constituted as per the registered deed No. 17/IV/2024"
column5: Trust Deed No. 2/IV/2007 · Cheppad SRO · 04.01.2007 — and D2's own admission, O.S. 214/2025 · reply to IA 5/2025 · 16.02.2026 · para 3: trust "registered on 04-01-2007"
column6: The trust is pleaded as founded on an unauthorised 2024 amendment rather than on its 2007 constituting deed.
column7: The deed pleaded (No. 17/IV/2024) is not the trust's constituting deed (2/IV/2007), per R2's own later filings.

column3: Unauthorised amendments to the trust
column4: Trust Deed No. 2/IV/2007 · 04.01.2007 (certified): clauses 1–16 reserve no power to amend, vary or revoke the trust or its beneficiary class
column5: Defendants' conduct: two registered deeds — 66/IV/2022 and 17/IV/2024 — altering the beneficiary class and enlarging trustee powers
column6: The trust was twice amended unilaterally, with no power to amend and without the consent or notice of the beneficiaries.
column7: The deed reserves no power to amend or revoke; cf. ITA 1882 ss.11 & 78. Amendments challenged as void in O.S. 214/2025.

column3: Plaintiff sworn to be "not a beneficiary"
column4: O.S. 214/2025 · reply to IA 5/2025 · 16.02.2026 · para 11: "The plaintiff is not a beneficiary"
column5: Trust Deed No. 2/IV/2007 · 04.01.2007 · p.3: beneficiaries are "members of the Alumoottil family who are the descendents of Alumoottil Chellamma Channathy"; the deed records the five brothers (incl. Radhakrishnan) as her sons; the defendants admit the plaintiff is "S/o Radhakrishnan Channar" (O.S. 243/2024, cause-title)
column6: The defendants admit the plaintiff is "S/o Radhakrishnan Channar" (O.S. 243/2024); the 2007 deed records Radhakrishnan as a son of Chellamma Channathy — placing the plaintiff within the beneficiary class.
column7: Inconsistent with the registered founding deed and the defendants' admission of the plaintiff's parentage.

column3: Beneficiary branded a stranger and trespasser
column4: O.S. 243/2024 · plaint by D2 · 14.08.2024 · para 4: plaintiff "is not a member" and "doesn't have any connection with the plaintiff trust"
column5: Trust Deed No. 2/IV/2007 · 04.01.2007 · p.3: beneficiaries are "members of the Alumoottil family who are the descendents of … Chellamma Channathy" (the plaintiff is admittedly "S/o Radhakrishnan"; the deed records Radhakrishnan as her son)
column6: A beneficiary under the deed is pleaded to the court as a stranger to the trust. [The plaintiff resided in the ancestral home as a child — provable from BBLPS Nangiarkulangara records (std I–IV), photographs and classmate affidavits.]
column7: Inconsistent with the beneficiary class in the registered founding deed.

column3: Cause of action of O.S. 243/2024 fabricated — plaintiff had left India
column4: O.S. 243/2024 · plaint by D2 · 14.08.2024 · paras 5–6: cause of action arose on 13.08.2024 on "reliable information … that the defendant is making hasty preparations to trespass into the plaint scheduled property again" (on/after that date)
column5: Plaintiff's passport (Document 10, O.S. 214/2025) — Indian immigration exit stamp dated 10.08.2024; departure from India early on 11.08.2024 (entry 02.08.2024; no further 2024 movement)
column6: The plaintiff was stamped out on 10.08.2024 and left India in the early hours of 11.08.2024; he was abroad on 13.08.2024 and could not have been preparing a trespass on or after that date.
column7: The pleaded cause of action (13.08.2024) post-dates the plaintiff's departure from India; contradicted by the immigration record.

column3: D5's two capacities: "Meda Construction Supervisor" vs. "no connection"
column4: Crime No. 574/2024 (now C.C. 1055/2024), Kareelakulangara PS — complaint by D5 (Arun B. Unni), who describes himself as "Alummoottil Meda Construction Supervisor"
column5: O.S. 214/2025 · objection of D5 (verified by Arun B. Unni) · 18.02.2026 · para 3: respondents "5, 6, 7 have no connection with Alummoottil Tharavadu Trust"; D5 "is not having direct connection with the trust or trustees" and "was the worker of the contractor who had taken work for the 3rd respondent"
column6: D5 holds the title "Alummoottil Meda Construction Supervisor" to prosecute a beneficiary over events at the trust property, while swearing he has no connection with the trust when its accounts are sought.
column7: Self-contradictory sworn statements by D5. The plaintiff is the accused in C.C. 1055/2024 (arrest stayed by the High Court of Kerala in Crl.M.C. No. 5800 of 2025).

column3: "Trust employees" are in fact D3's contractor's workers
column4: O.S. 243/2024 · plaint by D2 · 14.08.2024 · para 4: "employees deployed by the plaintiff trust"
column5: O.S. 214/2025 · objections of D5, D6 & D7 · 18.02.2026 · para 3: the workers were "the contractor's," engaged "for the 3rd respondent," with "no connection" to the trust
column6: Persons pleaded by D2 as the trust's employees swear they were a contractor's workers engaged for D3 (Sivadasan) personally.
column7: Mutually exclusive sworn statements; the works are placed under D3 personally.

column3: Dormant trust said to have deployed employees
column4: O.S. 243/2024 · plaint by D2 · 14.08.2024 · para 4: the plaintiff "manhandled … employees deployed by the plaintiff trust"
column5: O.S. 214/2025 · reply to IA 5/2025 · 16.02.2026 · paras 4–7: the trust had "no funding … or … revenue from any source whatsoever" and was a "dormant entity"
column6: A trust pleaded to have no funds or operations is said to have deployed and paid its own employees.
column7: Mutually exclusive sworn statements. [Tie the employees' engagement to the dormancy period claimed.]

column3: D6 and D7: "no connection," yet present at the premises
column4: O.S. 214/2025 · objection of D6 & D7 (verified) · 18.02.2026 · para 3: respondents "5, 6, 7 have no connection with Alummoottil Tharavadu Trust"
column5: Crime No. 956/2024, Kareelakulangara PS (complaint by the plaintiff against D2, D3, D5, D6, D7 and another), with photograph(s): D6 and D7 present within the trust premises during the 09.08.2024 incident
column6: D6 and D7 swear they have no connection to the trust, yet are named as present within the trust premises during the 09.08.2024 incident.
column7: Mutually exclusive accounts. [Crime 956/2024 status to be confirmed; photographs to be exhibited.]

column3: Documents sworn "not available," then produced
column4: O.S. 214/2025 · reply to IA 5/2025 · 16.02.2026 · para 13: the FY 2025–26 documents demanded "is not available for production before the court"
column5: O.S. 214/2025 · D2 affidavit · 18.05.2026 · para 3: D2 produces the a/c 13100200004482 statement (28.05.2025–15.05.2026), the Original Minutes Book and resolutions
column6: Having sworn the documents were unavailable, the deponent produced them once confronted in the rejoinder.
column7: Contradicted by the deponent's own subsequent production.

column3: Lease deed withheld, then produced
column4: O.S. 214/2025 · reply to IA 5/2025 · 16.02.2026 · paras 8 & 13: FY 2025–26 records described as the 26/05/2025 account and passbook, the rest "not available"
column5: O.S. 214/2025 · D2 affidavit · 18.05.2026 · para 3: a lease deed D2 himself executed on 27.10.2025 with Sree Moolavasam Pvt. Ltd., now produced
column6: A lease executed in the deponent's own hand was omitted from his sworn account of the trust's records.
column7: A registered instrument within the deponent's knowledge, omitted from his account of the trust's records and produced later.

column3: Production timed against the High Court's time-bound disposal order
column4: High Court · OP(C) 3278/2025 · order 15.01.2026: IA 2, 5 & 9 of 2025 to be disposed within three months (i.e. by ~15.04.2026)
column5: D2's reply (16.02.2026) stated the demanded documents were "not available"; on that footing IA 5/2025 was closed "in light of the affidavit" (order 04.03.2026); the Sreemoolavasam lease and the other records were produced only on 18.05.2026 — the first sitting after the court's summer recess
column6: IA 5/2025 (production) was closed within the High Court's three-month window on a sworn "not available" footing, yet the same records (including the lease) were produced afterwards, on 18.05.2026, as the defendants' own evidence.
column7: The "not available" affidavit was used to close the production application within the High Court's three-month window; the records — including the lease — were produced only after it ran. The closure was procured by a "not available" affidavit later shown to be untrue, so compliance with the High Court's direction was nominal, not real, and the production was timed to defeat its effect.

column3: Undisclosed health-care business on the trust premises
column4: O.S. 214/2025 · reply to IA 5/2025 · 16.02.2026 · paras 8 & 13: FY 2025–26 disclosure limited to the passbook, the rest "not available"
column5: GST extract · GSTIN 32ABQCS9416G1Z1 · effective 09.12.2025: Sree Moolavasam Wellness & Leisure Retreats Pvt. Ltd. (incorporated 07.08.2025 — 56 days after the suit was filed on 12.06.2025), status Active, place of business = the trust premises (2/242 Alumoottil, Muttom, Haripad, 690511), residential health-care services
column6: A GST-registered health-care business operates on the trust premises during the very period for which disclosure was limited to a passbook.
column7: Sworn statement inconsistent with a Government (GST) record.

column3: Construction: the trust's project, or D3's personal project?
column4: O.S. 243/2024 · plaint by D2 · 14.08.2024 · paras 3–4: construction "at the instance of the plaintiff trust"
column5: O.S. 214/2025 · reply to IA 5/2025 · 16.02.2026 · paras 6–7: the trust was "dormant"; per the 12.02.2020 resolution D3 alone funded the works "from his personal accounts"
column6: The works are the trust's in one suit and D3's personal activity in the other.
column7: Mutually exclusive accounts on oath. cf. ITA 1882 s.66 (blending of trust property).

column3: No accounts kept for ~18 years despite a mandatory duty
column4: Trust Deed No. 2/IV/2007 · 04.01.2007 · clause 2 (p.4): "The Managing Trustee also bound to maintain all records and accounts of the trust"
column5: O.S. 214/2025 · reply to IA 5/2025 · 16.02.2026 · paras 4–5: "no books of account were maintained" for FY 2007–2024
column6: The Managing Trustee admits keeping no accounts for ~18 years, contrary to the duty imposed by the deed itself.
column7: Inconsistent with the accounts duty in 2007 deed cl.2; cf. ITA 1882 ss.19, 57, 23.

column3: Lease registered outside the jurisdictional registry
column4: O.S. 214/2025 · D2 affidavit · 18.05.2026 · para 3: the lease was "registered through the Marad, Alappuzha Sub-Registrar's Office"
column5: Record: the trust property and the founding deed (2/IV/2007) lie within Cheppad SRO, Alappuzha (Maradu is in Ernakulam district); and by WP(C) 31247/2025, order 27.08.2025, the High Court directed Cheppad SRO to furnish the plaintiff certified copies of the trust deeds
column6: The lease (27.10.2025) was registered outside Cheppad SRO — the registry with jurisdiction over the trust property, and the one the High Court had, on 27.08.2025, directed to furnish the plaintiff certified copies of the trust deeds.
column7: Registered outside the Cheppad SRO jurisdiction — the registry with jurisdiction over the trust property, and the one the High Court had on 27.08.2025 directed to furnish the plaintiff certified copies — the lease was registered elsewhere to evade scrutiny at that registry. Jurisdiction in any event seriously questionable.

column3: Bank account opening date
column5: O.S. 214/2025 · D2 affidavit · 18.05.2026 · para 3: the same account opened "28/05/2025"
column6: Two sworn opening dates for the trust's first bank account.
column7: Reconcile against the bank record.

column3: Date error in the evidence affidavit
column4: O.S. 214/2025 · D2 affidavit · 18.05.2026 · para 3: statement of account "from 28/05/2005 …"
column5: Same para: the account was opened 28/05/2025
column6: The affidavit dates the statement to 2005, before the account existed.
column7: Obtain a corrected copy for the evidence side.

column3: Managing Trustee's age
column6: Stated age rises 77 → 78 → 79 within ~3 months.
column7: Particulars only.

column3: Managing Trustee's parentage
column4: O.S. 214/2025 · reply to IA 5/2025 · 16.02.2026: "S/o Madhavan Thurail Krishnan Channar"
column5: other O.S. 214/2025 filings: "S/o late T.K. Madhavan"
